stem: A car is purchased for $\$18{,}000$. Stamp duty is charged at 3% of the purchase price. What is the stamp duty payable (in dollars)?
hint: Multiply the purchase price by the stamp duty rate: $0.03 \times 18{,}000$.

stem: A car is purchased for $\$25{,}000$. Stamp duty is charged at 3% of the purchase price. What is the stamp duty payable (in dollars)?
hint: Convert 3% to a decimal and multiply by the purchase price.

stem: A vehicle is purchased for $\$32{,}500$. Stamp duty is 3% of the purchase price. What is the stamp duty payable (in dollars)?
A vehicle is purchased for $\$45{,}000$. Stamp duty is charged at 3% of the purchase price. What is the stamp duty payable (in dollars)?
A car has a purchase price of $\$22{,}000$. Stamp duty is 3% of the purchase price, registration costs $\$350$ and CTP insurance costs $\$600$. What is the total on-road cost (in dollars)?
hint: Calculate stamp duty first, then add purchase price, registration and CTP insurance.

stem: A vehicle costs $\$38{,}000$. Stamp duty is 3% of the purchase price, registration costs $\$420$ and CTP insurance costs $\$780$. What is the total on-road cost (in dollars)?
hint: Find 3% of $\$38{,}000$ for stamp duty, then add all four amounts.

stem: A used car is purchased for $\$15{,}000$. Stamp duty is 3% of the purchase price, registration costs $\$290$ and CTP insurance costs $\$530$. What is the total on-road cost (in dollars)?
hint: Stamp duty $= 0.03 \times 15{,}000$. Then add all costs.

stem: A vehicle is purchased for $\$54{,}000$. Stamp duty is 3% of the purchase price, registration costs $\$480$ and CTP insurance costs $\$860$. What is the total on-road cost (in dollars)?
hint: Find 3% of $\$54{,}000$ for stamp duty, then add all four amounts.

stem: A car has a cash price of $\$28{,}000$. A loan option requires a $\$5{,}000$ deposit and 48 monthly repayments of $\$560$. What is the total amount paid under the loan option (in dollars)?
hint: Total loan cost $=$ deposit $+$ (number of repayments $\times$ repayment amount).

stem: A car has a cash price of $\$28{,}000$. A loan option requires a $\$5{,}000$ deposit and 48 monthly repayments of $\$560$. What is the total interest paid under the loan option (in dollars)?
hint: Find the total loan cost first, then subtract the cash price.

stem: A car has a cash price of $\$19{,}500$. Under a finance deal, the buyer pays a $\$3{,}000$ deposit and 36 monthly repayments of $\$510$. What is the total cost of the finance option (in dollars)?
hint: Total cost $=$ deposit $+$ total of all monthly repayments.

stem: A car has a cash price of $\$35{,}000$. A loan option requires a $\$7{,}000$ deposit and 60 monthly repayments of $\$530$. What is the total interest paid expressed as a percentage of the cash price? Give your answer rounded to the nearest whole number.
hint: Find the total loan cost, subtract the cash price to get interest, then calculate $\dfrac{\text{interest}}{\text{cash price}} \times 100$.
